_comment: PixelLab generated the tileset structure; local palette clamping creates the strict black/white and gameplay-green halves of the showcase image.
_comment_prompt: /pixellab-pip create 1-bit tileset with black upper, black lower, and black transition with horizontal white stripes. after done, create a copy with gameplay 1 bit green colors.

MCP create_topdown_tileset.lower_description: solid black 1-bit terrain, pure black fill, flat untextured surface, no gray tones
MCP create_topdown_tileset.upper_description: solid black 1-bit terrain, pure black fill, flat untextured surface, no gray tones
MCP create_topdown_tileset.transition_description: solid black transition bands with crisp horizontal pure white stripes, high contrast black and white only, no gray tones

TASK.instruction: Use the 64x64 tileset image returned by the immediately preceding PixelLab call. Palette-clamp one native-size copy to exactly #000000 and #FFFFFF. Remap that accepted black/white copy to a second native-size copy using #0F380F for black and #9BBC0F for white. Place the black/white copy on the left and the gameplay-green copy on the right without scaling or shape edits.
TASK.verify: The output is exactly 128x64; each half is 64x64 and shape/alpha-identical; the left uses only #000000/#FFFFFF and the right uses only #0F380F/#9BBC0F for opaque pixels.
